cells.cell_text: Mean CD4 count per μl of blood (range)
Mean viral load copies per ml (range)

page.content: In the study categorized by SM+ and SM−, the No. of individuals (n) for Female in SM+ is 3, while for Male it is 15; in SM−, Female has 11 and Male has 9. The Mean age in years (range) for SM+ Female is 28 (21–39), for SM+ Male it is 29 (23–41), for SM− Female it is 27 (19–45), and for SM− Male it is 30 (24–37). The Mean CD4 count per μl of blood (range) for SM+ Female is 580 (361–779), for SM+ Male it is 782 (362–1,228), for SM− Female it is 701 (436–1,115), and for SM− Male it is 775 (417–2,000). The Mean viral load copies per ml (range) for SM+ Female is 4,349 (2,408–5,957), for SM+ Male it is 138,317 (2,664–786,051), for SM− Female it is 185,047 (248–830,432), and for SM− Male it is 47,294 (20–104,879). Additionally, in Table 2, the Specie of Human contains Fat at 11 g/L, Protein at 42 g/L, and Lactose at 70 g/L; Cow has Fat ranging from 35–45 g/L, Protein from 30–36 g/L, and Lactose from 47–50 g/L; Sheep has Fat from 60–80 g/L, Protein from 50–65 g/L, and Lactose from 44–48 g/L; Goat has Fat from 30–34 g/L, Protein from 27–37 g/L, and Lactose from 42–48 g/L; Buffalo has Fat from 70–74 g/L, Protein from 38–44 g/L, and Lactose from 48–50 g/L.
